scenarios.title: The Air Canada Chatbot
scenarios.headline: The chatbot that made up a bereavement policy
scenarios.summary: Air Canada's customer service chatbot told a grieving customer he could book a full-fare ticket and apply for a bereavement discount retroactively. No such policy existed. The Canadian tribunal ruled the airline liable.
scenarios.failure_mode: Ungrounded generation — the model hallucinated policy from training data instead of retrieving from the actual policy database.

scenarios.title: The M-Pesa Holiday Freeze
scenarios.headline: The fraud detection that froze mama mbogas at Christmas
scenarios.summary: Safaricom's automated fraud detection flagged thousands of legitimate small-business M-Pesa transactions as suspicious during the December holiday rush. Mama mbogas, boda boda drivers, and small traders had accounts frozen with no explanation.
scenarios.failure_mode: Training distribution mismatch — the model's idea of normal didn't include seasonal informal commerce surges.

scenarios.title: The UK Visa Streaming Tool
scenarios.headline: The 'racist algorithm' the UK Home Office had to scrap
scenarios.summary: The Home Office used an algorithmic visa risk scoring tool that gave Nigerian and other Global South applicants disproportionately high risk scores. The Joint Council for the Welfare of Immigrants sued. Scrapped August 2020.
scenarios.failure_mode: Historical bias inherited through training data.

scenarios.title: The Amazon Recruiting Tool
scenarios.headline: The hiring AI that learned to reject women
scenarios.summary: Amazon's experimental recruiting AI taught itself to penalise resumes containing 'women's'. Trained on a decade of male-dominated resumes, it inherited the imbalance. Scrapped in 2017.
scenarios.failure_mode: Encoded historical bias as a feature.

scenarios.title: The Apple Card Credit Limit Scandal
scenarios.headline: The black box that gave husbands 20x more credit than wives
scenarios.summary: DHH and Wozniak both reported Apple Card gave them dramatically higher credit limits than their wives despite shared finances. Goldman Sachs's defence collapsed when investigators showed the model couldn't explain its decisions.

scenarios.title: The Ghana Voter Verification Failure
scenarios.headline: The biometric system that couldn't read worn fingerprints
scenarios.summary: Ghana's 2020 election biometric verification failed for legitimate voters — particularly older women whose fingerprints had worn down from decades of farm and market work. Some were turned away before manual overrides.
scenarios.failure_mode: Model never tested on the population it would actually serve.

scenarios.title: The Zindi Cassava Disease Model
scenarios.headline: The 95% accurate model that failed in real fields
scenarios.summary: A computer vision model trained on a Zindi competition dataset for cassava disease achieved 95% accuracy on the test set. When farmers in northern Ghana used it via WhatsApp, real-world accuracy dropped below 40%. The model had learned the backgrounds of training photos as much as the diseases.

scenarios.title: The Lagos Surge Pricing Trap
scenarios.headline: The ride-hailing app that profited from terror
scenarios.summary: During the EndSARS protests, a major ride-hailing app's surge pricing charged users up to 10x normal fares as people fled dangerous areas. The algorithm responded to demand exactly as designed — but had no awareness the spike was caused by people fleeing violence.

scenarios.title: The Western Cape Healthcare Triage Model
scenarios.headline: The model that under-triaged township patients
scenarios.summary: A machine learning model deployed in Western Cape hospitals systematically under-prioritised township patients. It used postcode as a proxy and learned that township patients had historically received less aggressive treatment.

scenarios.title: The Kenya Digital Lenders
scenarios.headline: The 7-second loan that became algorithmic shaming at scale
scenarios.summary: Tala, Branch, Okash approved microloans in seconds based on phone data. When borrowers fell behind, the system SMS-blasted their entire contact list demanding payment. Annualised rates often exceeded 600%. The Central Bank of Kenya regulated in 2022.
scenarios.failure_mode: Speed and scale without recoverability or human-in-the-loop.

scenarios.title: The Meta Ethiopia Moderation Failure
scenarios.headline: Twenty-five Amharic moderators for a continent at war
scenarios.summary: During the Tigray war, a Tigrayan professor was named in a viral Facebook post and later murdered. Court filings revealed Meta had only 25 Amharic content moderators for over 500 million African users. The automated moderation had been trained primarily on English.
scenarios.failure_mode: Language and context blindness in the safety layer.
